title: Anatomy of a Biological Neuron
description: A visual breakdown of the primary components of a biological neuron, showing the flow of information from input to output.
relationships: Dendrites receive electrical signals from other neurons and carry them toward the Soma. The Soma processes these inputs. If the combined signal exceeds a threshold, the Axon transmits an action potential. The Myelin Sheath insulates the Axon to speed up signal propagation, which jumps between the Nodes of Ranvier. The signal reaches the Axon Terminals, which release neurotransmitters into the Synapse to communicate with the Dendrites of the next neuron.
